symptom: Duty Cycle Exceeded

causes.check: Allow the machine to cool.

symptom: Low Voltage Input

causes.cause: The voltage input is below the specified limit
causes.check: Inspect input voltage and ensure it is within machine limits.

symptom: High Voltage Input

causes.cause: The voltage input is higher than the specified limit
causes.check: Inspect input voltage and ensure it is within machine limits.

symptom: Voltage Too Low or Wire Feed Too Slow

causes.cause: Voltage Too Low or Wire Feed Too Slow
causes.check: Increase Output Voltage or Increase Wire Feed Speed

symptom: Voltage Too High or Wire Feed Too Fast

causes.cause: Voltage Too High or Wire Feed Too Fast
causes.check: Decrease Output Voltage or Decrease Wire Feed Speed

symptom: Travel Speed Too Fast

causes.check: Travel slower

symptom: Travel Speed Too Slow

causes.check: Travel faster

symptom: CTWD Too Long or Wrong Polarity

causes.cause: CTWD Too Long or Wrong Polarity
causes.check: Check Polarity and maintain less than 1/2" CTWD

symptom: Wire Weld–Porosity (Small cavities or holes in the bead)

causes.check: Check that polarity is set correctly for type of welding.
Increase flow of gas. Clean nozzle. Maintain proper CTWD.
Use shielding gas recommended by wire supplier.
Clean workpiece down to bare metal. Make certain that wire is clean and free from oil, coatings, and other residues.
Maintain steady travel speed.
Reduce CTWD.

symptom: Wire Weld–Excessive Spatter (Spatter that is grainy and large is a problem)

causes.check: Clean workpiece down to bare metal. Make certain that wire is clean and free from oil, coatings, and other residues.
Check that polarity is set correctly for type of welding.
Increase flow of gas. Clean nozzle. Maintain proper CTWD.
Reduce wire feed speed.
Reduce CTWD.

symptom: Wire Weld–Crooked/Wavy Bead

causes.check: Use two hands or rest hand on steady surface.
Maintain steady travel speed.
Reduce CTWD.

symptom: Wire Weld–Burn-Through (Base material melts away, leaving a hole in the weld)

causes.check: Reduce current and/or wire feed speed.
Increase travel speed and ensure that travel speed is kept steady.
Reduce wire feed speed.

symptom: Stick Weld Penetration – Excess Penetration or Burn-Through

causes.check: Reduce current.
Increase welding speed and ensure that welding speed is kept steady.

symptom: Stick Weld Penetration – Inadequate Penetration

causes.check: Keep arc on leading edge of weld puddle. Hold torch at proper angles.
Slow down so fill material has time to melt into the weld location. Increase current.
Bevel thick workpieces, allow slight gap, and weld in several passes.
Increase amount of fill material.

symptom: Stick Weld – Weld Not Adhering Properly (gaps present between weld and previous bead or between weld and workpiece)

causes.check: Place stringer bead at correct place in joint. Adjust workpiece position or weld angle to permit proper welding to bottom of piece. Keep arc on leading edge of weld puddle. Hold Electrode and fill material at proper angles.
Increase current.
Clean workpiece down to bare metal.
Increase amount of fill material.
Decrease distance and increase bevel.

symptom: Stick Weld – Bend at Joint

causes.check: Clamp workpieces securely. Make tack welds to hold workpieces.
Weld a small portion and allow to cool before proceeding. Increase weld speed.

symptom: Stick Weld – Porosity — Small cavities or holes in the bead

causes.check: Clean workpiece down to bare metal. Make certain that fill material and Electrode are clean and free from oil, coatings, and other residues.
Maintain steady weld speed.

symptom: Stick Weld – Crooked/Wavy Bead

causes.check: Use two hands or rest hand on steady surface.
Maintain steady weld speed.

symptom: Stick Weld – Excessive Spatter

causes.check: Clean workpiece down to bare metal. Make certain that fill material and Electrode are clean and free from oil, coatings, and other residues.

symptom: Stick Weld – Burn-Through — Base material melts away, leaving a hole in the weld

causes.check: Reduce current.
Increase welding speed and ensure that welding speed is kept steady.
Reduce amount of fill material.

symptom: Wire Feed Motor Runs but Wire Does Not Feed Properly

causes.check: Increase wire feed pressure properly. Follow step 27 on page 17.
Flip roll to correct size. Follow the Feed Roller instructions on page 12.

causes.cause: Damaged MIG Gun, cable, or liner assembly.
causes.check: Have a qualified technician inspect these parts and replace as necessary.
Loosen Feed Tensioner so it applies only enough pressure to prevent continued spinning after the Gun Trigger is released.

symptom: Wire Creates a Bird's Nest During Operation

causes.check: Adjust wire feed pressure properly. Follow step 27 on page 17.
Replace with the proper tip for wire used.

causes.cause: MIG Gun Cable Connector not fully inserted into Wire Feed mechanism.
causes.check: Insert Gun Cable Connector properly. Follow steps 13 and 14 on page 13.
Have a qualified technician inspect and repair/replace as necessary.

symptom: Wire Stops During Welding

causes.cause: Gun cable is severely bent and Wire Feed mechanism cannot feed wire.
causes.check: Straighten Gun cable.

causes.cause: Gun liner is clogged or worn.
causes.check: Check gun liner for obstruction. Replace if necessary.

causes.cause: Gun liner is too small for welding wire being used.
causes.check: Check that gun liner is correct size for wire.

causes.cause: Wire is tangled on the spool.
causes.check: Check wire for cross winding or tangled spool.

causes.cause: Wire is not making contact with Feed Rollers.
causes.check: Check Feed Rollers and ensure correct groove for wire diameter is being used.

causes.cause: Feed Roller is not making enough contact with wire or is crushing flux-cored wire.
causes.check: Check Feed Tensioner and ensure it is set properly.

symptom: Welding Arc Not Stable

causes.check: See first Troubleshooting section above.

causes.cause: Incorrect Contact Tip or liner size or excessive wear.
causes.check: Replace with the proper tip or liner size for wire used.
Adjust wire feed speed to achieve a more stable arc.

causes.cause: Loose MIG Gun cable or ground cable.
causes.check: Check to ensure that all connections are tight.

causes.cause: Damaged MIG Gun or loose connection within Gun.
causes.check: Have a qualified technician inspect and repair/replace as necessary.

causes.cause: Incorrect polarity for process being run.
causes.check: Ensure polarity is correct for operation: DCEP for MIG welding and DCEN for Flux-Cored self-shielded welding.

causes.cause: Gas coverage may be insufficient or too high.
causes.check: Ensure gas flow rate is set according to Settings Chart. Make sure MIG Gun Cable Connector is fully inserted into Wire Feed mechanism with no O-Rings exposed.
Check the ground clamp connection to the workpiece and machine. Ensure the MIG Gun is properly secured.

symptom: Weak Arc Strength

causes.check: Check the line voltage and, if insufficient, have a licensed electrician remedy the situation.

causes.cause: Improper gauge or length of cord.
causes.check: Do not use an extension cord on this Welder. Use only one of the supplied power cords for this Welder or an identical replacement cord.
Switch current to proper setting for metal thickness.

symptom: Welder Does Not Function When Switched On

causes.check: If a warning screen appears in the LCD Display window, Welder may have overheated and shut down. Stop and wait with the Power Switch ON for the Welder to cool. The Welder automatically returns to service after cooling off. Reduce duration or frequency of welding periods to help reduce wear on the Welder. Refer to Duty Cycle (Duration of Use) on page 19.

causes.cause: Circuit supplies insufficient input voltage or amperage.
causes.check: Verify that the circuit is designed to supply the required input voltage and amperage as detailed on the Specifications table. If a warning screen appears in the LCD Display window, check the input voltage to ensure it is within specified range.
Ensure the gun connection is properly seated on machine. Qualified technician must check and secure/replace Trigger.

causes.cause: Machine is in low- or over-voltage protection.
causes.check: Check input voltage and ensure it falls within the specified range. If input voltage is correct, press Reset Button on back of machine.

causes.cause: Machine is in the incorrect mode.
causes.check: Ensure the correct process has been selected.

symptom: LCD Display Does Not Light When Welder is Switched On

causes.cause: Unit is not connected to outlet properly.
causes.check: Verify the voltage at the outlet and the connection to the outlet.
Check circuit breaker/GFCI devices; if any are tripped, determine and remedy cause before resetting.

causes.cause: Plug does not have correct rating.
causes.check: Make sure installed plug is correct rating. See Specifications on page 7.

causes.cause: Circuit breaker has tripped due to high input amperage.
causes.check: Press Reset Button on back of machine to reset circuit breaker.

causes.cause: Input Power Cord is not seated properly.
causes.check: Ensure the twist lock input Power Cord is fully secured.

symptom: Wire Feeds, but Arc Does Not Ignite

causes.check: Make certain that the workpiece is contacted properly by the Ground Clamp and that the workpiece is properly cleaned near the ground clamp and the welding location.
Verify that Contact Tip is the proper size for welding wire. If needed, replace Contact Tip with proper size and type.
Check that the hole in the tip is not deformed or enlarged. If needed, replace Contact Tip with proper size and type.
Properly clean Contact Tip.

symptom: Porosity in the Weld Metal

causes.check: Check gas bottle and replenish as necessary.

causes.cause: Not enough or too much shielding gas.
causes.check: Check gas regulator to ensure proper flow.
Clean workpiece down to bare metal.

causes.cause: Gun is being used too far away from workpiece.
causes.check: Check CTWD (contact tip to work distance) for the proper procedure.

causes.cause: Polarity is incorrect for the application.
causes.check: Check the polarity and ensure it is DCEP for MIG and DCEN for Flux-Cored.

causes.cause: Dirty welding wire is introducing contamination into the weld.
causes.check: Make certain that welding wire is clean and free of rust and residues.

symptom: Welder Does Not Function When Switched On

causes.check: Reduce duration or frequency of welding periods to help reduce wear on the welder. Refer to Duty Cycle (Duration of Use) on page 29.
Qualified technician must check and secure/replace Trigger.

causes.cause: Ground Clamp not attached to workpiece.
causes.check: Attach Ground Clamp to workpiece.
Connect shielding gas to Welder.

symptom: LCD Display Does Not Light When Welder is Switched On

causes.cause: Unit is not connected to outlet properly.
causes.check: Verify the voltage at the outlet and the connection to the outlet.

Check circuit breaker/GFCI devices; if any are tripped, determine and remedy cause before resetting. Verify that the circuit is designed to supply the required input amperage as detailed in Specifications on page 7.

symptom: Weak Arc Strength

causes.check: Check the line voltage and, if insufficient, have a licensed electrician remedy the situation.

causes.cause: Improper gauge or length of cord.
causes.check: Do not use an extension cord on this Welder. Use only one of the supplied power cords for this Welder or an identical replacement cord.

symptom: Welding Arc Not Stable.

causes.cause: Loose electrode cable or ground cable.
causes.check: Check to ensure that all connections are tight.

causes.cause: Damaged electrode holder or loose connection within electrode holder.
causes.check: Have a qualified technician inspect and repair/replace as necessary.
Make sure setting matches recommended setting on chart.
Replace shielding gas cylinder.
